audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for governing authorities, taxes, love fulfilling Law, wakefulness. Include caution against absolutizing empire obedience.

verses.restored: Let every soul/life be subject to higher authorities; for there is no authority except under the Divine, and those existing have been arranged under the Divine.
Therefore the one resisting the authority has opposed the arrangement of the Divine, and those opposing will receive judgment for themselves.
For rulers are not a fear to the good work but to the harmful. Do you wish not to fear authority? Do the good, and you will have praise from it.

For it is servant of the Divine to you for the good. But if you do the harmful, fear; for it does not bear the sword without purpose, for it is servant of the Divine, an avenger into wrath to the one practicing harm.
Therefore it is necessary to be subject, not only because of wrath but also because of conscience.
For because of this you also pay taxes, for they are public servants of the Divine, attending to this very thing.
Give back to all what is owed: tax to whom tax, tribute to whom tribute, fear/reverence to whom fear/reverence, honor to whom honor.
Owe nothing to anyone except to love one another; for the one loving the other has fulfilled Law.

For the commandments, You shall not commit adultery, you shall not murder, you shall not steal, you shall not desire, and if there is any other commandment, are summed up in this word: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
Love does not work harm to the neighbor; therefore love is fulfillment of Law.
And this, knowing the season, that it is already the hour for you to awaken from sleep; for now our salvation/healing is nearer than when we trusted.
The night advanced, and the day has drawn near. Therefore let us put away the works of darkness, and let us put on the weapons of light.
Let us walk beautifully/properly as in the day, not in revelry and drunkenness, not in bedchambers and sensual excess, not in strife and jealousy.
But put on the Lord Jesus Messiah, and make no forethought for flesh into desires.

safety_note: Romans 13:1-7 should not be absolutized into obedience to unjust state violence. It follows Romans 12's non-retaliation ethic and is bounded by Paul's wider witness that Divine authority judges authorities too. The chapter's center moves into love fulfilling Law and wakeful light.
